Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Pltf To Compel Further Answers At Deposition From Jason Alexander Coffman
Off Calendar May-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Robert Taitz, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Stephen Schreiver
J. Michael S. Onderick, for Defendant and opposing party: Bayside Village Associates

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Stephen Schreiber's motion to compel further responses at deposition is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to questions #1, 3 - 6. The motion is denied as to questions #2 and 7.
Deposition to be completed by June 30, 208. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records, Or In The Alternative, For A Protective Order
Off Calendar May-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
J. Michael S. Onderick, for Defendant and moving party: Bayside Village Associates and Jason Coffman
Robert Taitz, Esq., for Plaintiff and opposing party: Stephen Schreiver

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted with modification. Defendant Bayside Village Associates and Jason Coffman's motion for a protective order. The motion is moot as to Ambassador Security, Wells Fargo Security, United Parcel Service, and Woodrow Wilson High School as the subpoenas have been withdrawn. The subpoenas to Kaiser Hospital and Kaiser PMG are modified to only require production of records relating to any treatment from April 18, 2006 to the present for injuries sustained to the hand, back, and nose. The subpoena to San Francisco Patrol, Special Police Officers Association is modified to only require production of records relating to the April 18, 2006 incident at Bayside Village.
MODIFICATION. As to San Francisco Patrol to also require disclosure of records form April 1, 2001 re: incidents of use of force where the witness was involved.
In addition, any medical records shall be sumitted to counsel for initial review. Any redactions shall cause the parties to meet and confer and the Court may conduct an in Camera review.
Compliance within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.
